summary: ForgeSlicer iteration 4 testing. Backend pytest: 14/14 pass (8 gallery + 6 community-printer CRUD). Frontend: dynamic Send-to-Slicer split button verified for Custom/Generic (OrcaSlicer + alt prusa/cura), FlashForge AD5X (FlashPrint 5 + orca_ff/orca), Bambu X1C (Bambu Studio + orca), Prusa MK4 (PrusaSlicer + orca/superslicer), and FlashForge Finder (FlashPrint 5, NO dropdown). Clicking the SuperSlicer alternate opens the OrcaDialog with title 'SEND TO SUPERSLICER' — title and slicer name correctly substituted. Manifold warning chip (data-testid='manifold-warning') correctly hidden for empty scene and single-positive cylinder; appears with positive cylinder + negative torus showing '994 open edges' and reassurance 'Your print will still slice fine — modern slicers (OrcaSlicer, PrusaSlicer, FlashPrint 5, Bambu Studio) all auto-repair on import' (~700ms after CSG); disappears when negative torus is deleted. Save-printer community dialog opens. Undo/redo/measure/grid/snap toolbar buttons all present. No console pageerrors. No backend or frontend issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: RightPanel.jsx is ~630 lines and bundles Inspector/Profile/Slicer/Stats/Manifold/Compat — consider splitting per the >700 line guideline soon (still under threshold).
TopToolbar Send menu uses onBlur + setTimeout(150) to close — fragile; could close itself before click registers in slow envs. Works in tests but consider click-outside hook.
ManifoldHealth runs evaluateScene synchronously every 350ms after object changes; for large scenes could cause UI jank. Consider moving to a Web Worker (also noted in iteration 1 for the slicer).
ProfileSection.handlePrinter mutates slicer-settings (nozzle, speed) on every printer change even if user already customized — could silently overwrite tweaks. Minor UX.
Mongo _id properly excluded via projections — confirmed by tests.
seed_data_creation: TEST_-prefixed printers and gallery items created during pytest are cleaned up in teardown_module of each test file.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Add-primitive testids are 'add-{type}-positive-btn' and 'add-{type}-negative-btn' (e.g. add-cylinder-positive-btn, add-torus-negative-btn), NOT add-cylinder-btn. The Send-to-Slicer primary button always has testid 'send-to-orcaslicer-btn' regardless of visible text (intentional). Chevron testid 'send-slicer-menu-btn' only renders when alternateSlicers.length > 0. Dropdown options use 'send-slicer-option-{id}'. The community-printer dialog (data-testid='save-printer-dialog') intercepts pointer events when open — close via the visible X close button rather than Escape (Escape did not close it during tests). Workspace route is /workspace. drop-to-bed-btn requires a selected object in inspector to render.
rca of the issue: n/a — no issues found in this iteration.
